mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly increasing present value of future corporate cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.64% as of 2024-11-01 reflects recent Fed policy stance
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not statistically significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity investors price in lower corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth (GDPC1 declining quarter-over-quarter) combined with falling retail sales (RSAFS declining month-over-month) indicates weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices earnings forecasts downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines QoQ and RSAFS declines MoM simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 23386.733B (2024-07-01 vintage), RSAFS at 718867M (2024-10-01 vintage)
failure_condition: Interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward returns on GDPC1 and RSAFS interaction, or mean 60-day returns during joint contraction periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) over a 2-month window signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression for manufacturers, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors immediately reprrice earnings expectations for cyclical sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.2805 (2024-10-01 vintage), revised to 99.4695 indicating downward revision risk
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) combined with stable or declining inflation (CPIAUCSL growth < 2.5% annualized) signals a goldilocks labor market that supports consumer spending without triggering Fed tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in sustained earnings growth without rate risk.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 100k MoM and CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 159288k (2024-11-01 vintage), CPIAUCSL at 316.441 (2024-11-01 vintage)
regime_hint: goldilocks labor market with contained inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or mean 20-day returns during joint favorable conditions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) declining below 1.2 million annualized units signals weakening residential investment demand that reduces construction employment and consumer wealth effects, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices cyclical sector earnings.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.2 million annualized units
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 60-day forward returns following HOUST declines below threshold are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL YoY) remains above 3%, the Fed is in restrictive policy stance that suppresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and reduced corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression of forward returns on FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL interaction, or mean 20-day returns during joint restrictive conditions are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerating above 2% annualized combined with rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals robust supply-side expansion and labor productivity gains that support margin expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors price in earnings upside.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month growth > 2% annualized AND PAYEMS rises > 150k MoM
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with strong labor demand
failure_condition: Interaction term shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, or mean 60-day forward returns during joint expansion conditions are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
